mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03, or the positive return frequency in high-growth months does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of trading days when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or the negative return hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production indicate a contraction in both labor and goods markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where UNRATE increases and INDPRO decreases over a three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an accelerating consumer price index forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances during tightening regimes, or Rank IC is insignificantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day SPX forward returns turns negative, or the positive return hit rate drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1850 thousand units signals weakening construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when HOUST is below 1850, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4% combined with contracting industrial production create a high real cost of capital for the goods sector, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 60% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% and INDPRO declines over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production growth above 1.5 index points over three months signals improving operating leverage and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and SPX returns falls below 0.03.
